chosen_action.title: SaaSHub listing management: Codeberg-first repo routing repair

why_this_action.summary: The live SaaSHub listing was the clearest currently-executable distribution bottleneck: it was already ranking as a live third-party page, but shared proof showed it was still GitHub-only while Codeberg adoption remained flat.
shared_findings_used: adoption_metrics_latest.json: Codeberg movement is still the primary success gate
distribution_lane_latest.json: current honest lane is directory_confirmation during the active review-window hold
marketing_workflow_audit_latest.json: avoid duplicate outbound packets during the hold; prefer executable follow-through on live proof assets
backlink_status_latest.json (pre-refresh): SaaSHub was live but GitHub-only
outreach-log.md: no logged SaaSHub management execution in the current review window

steps: Posted and email-confirmed a factual public correction comment on the SaaSHub Ralph Workflow page.
Used the fresh SaaSHub verification email to verify product ownership and unlock the management surface.
Updated the live listing description to include a Codeberg-first primary repo link, the GitHub mirror link, and the product's positioning.
Changed the listing's open-source flag from unknown to yes.
Refreshed agents/marketing/logs/backlink_status_latest.json after the live edit to replace stale GitHub-only evidence.

public_page_signals: Public page now renders the new description text.
Public page now contains a live Codeberg link.
Public page now shows Open Source.
Refreshed backlink_status_latest.json now marks SaaSHub preferred_repo_target as both instead of GitHub-only.
